response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain experience (GISCollective founder, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and credible low-latency systems work at Sociomantic with DLang, which is conceptually adjacent to modern C++/Rust. However, C++ experience is limited and dated (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015), there is no Rust experience, and recent work has been TypeScript/Node-focused. Worth a screening call to assess C++/Rust ramp-up potential given the geospatial fit, but not a strong match on the primary language requirement.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS background spanning nearly a decade (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Systems-language fluency in DLang plus Linux server application experience
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ exposure; no Rust experience listed
No direct navigation/routing or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent stack (TS/Node, AWS) diverges from the C++-primary role; currently in a career gap
